entry.title: SAP Learning Journey – Administrating SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Since it was announced at SAP TechEd 2021, SAP offers digital learnings free of charge to everybody interested in SAP products on https://learning.sap.com. In this blog post, I want to present to you the learning journey Administrating SAP Business Technology Platform.

 

Introduction

SAP offers plenty of services to enhance your business in one central place: SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). This platform-as-a-service (PaaS) provides the central place to improve your handling of data and analytics, integrate data and processes, or develop and extend software by leveraging such SAP BTP services.

But before you can operate a service, you must define the basis like directories, subaccounts, and runtime environments. Users need authorizations and applications need connections to service provides. There are various configuration options to define your service landscape suitable for your business scenarios. All of this should also be observed and monitored concerning performance or costs.

This is exactly what this learning journey is about: Administrating the basis for operating SAP cloud services in SAP BTP. And yes, I chose the term “basis” on purpose for those people out there, who are right now on their journey from on-premise to cloud. Just take a seat in our learning journey.

The units in the current version are the following:

  1. Overview, Responsibilities and Guidance
  2. Architecture
  3. Identity and Access Management
  4. Configuration
  5. Connectivity
  6. Observability and Monitoring
  7. Cost and Usage Management

 

Content

1. Overview, Responsibilities and Guidance

Unit 1 describes shortly the SAP Business Technology Platform and its commercial models. Most importantly, it analyzes responsibilities and guidance resources for SAP BTP.

There is no exercise in this unit. If you want to know more, please check our learning journey Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform acting as a prerequisite.

2. Architecture

Unit 2 covers the SAP BTP account model consisting of global accounts, directories, and subaccounts. After service subscriptions, the runtime environments in SAP BTP are managed. Cloud Foundry and Kyma are enabled and (name-) spaces created to instantiate services in these runtimes. Finaly, the ABAP environment is enabled and the landscape portal is used to manage ABAP system hibernation.

The following is exercised:

  • Create Directories
  • Create a Subaccount in a Directory
  • Create Subaccounts with Default Provider and Region
  • Entitle a Service in a Directory
  • Entitle and Subscribe a Service in a Subaccount
  • Add a Service Plan for Cloud Foundry Environment
  • Enable and Configure a Cloud Foundry Environment
  • Create a Service Instance in a Cloud Foundry
  • Add a Service Plan for Kyma Environment
  • Enable a Kyma Runtime
  • Create a Namespace in a Kyma Runtime
  • Create a Service Instance in Kyma
  • Entitle and Create an ABAP Environment
  • Entitle and Create Web Access for ABAP
  • Create a Subaccount for Operation
  • Entitle, Create, and Authorize the Landscape Portal
  • Check ABAP System Details and Manage System Hibernation

3. Identity and Access Management

Unit 3 explains the identity and access management on SAP BTP on all levels and distinguishes business from platform users. How to manage users and authorizations on SAP BTP  with roles and role collections connected to identity providers is followed by a summary of the security concept for cloud native applications. This leads to the management of users and authorizations in SAP BTP environments.

The following is exercised:

  • Create Administrator User in a Subaccount
  • Inspect and Assign Predefined Role Collections for Applications
  • Assign Developer Authorizations in Cloud Foundry
  • Manage Cluster Roles in Kyma
  • Manage Business Roles in the ABAP Environment

4. Configuration

Unit 4 provides various ways to configure the SAP BTP starting with boosters, which are guided interactive steps to configure services. Operating the command line interface (CLI) tools btp CLI for SAP BTP and cf CLI for Cloud Foundry are like the kubectl for Kyma the most direct way to interact with SAP BTP on a technical level. To manage SAP BTP with Terraform incorporates btp CLI to automate infrastructure provisioning and management.

The following is exercised:

  • Start a Booster in SAP BTP
  • Operate SAP BTP Command Line Interface (btp CLI)
  • Setting and Configuring Kubectl for Kyma
  • Create a Terraform Configuration for SAP BTP

5. Connectivity

Unit 5 starts with analyzing connectivity in SAP BTP and jumps to using destinations on subaccount, service, and environment level. To connect the SAP BTP to on-premise systems, operating the cloud connector is mandatory for any SAP BTP service.

The following is exercised:

  • Create a Destination
  • How to Install Cloud Connector on Linux
  • Replace the UI Certificate in Cloud Connector
  • How to Configure First SAP BTP Subaccount in Cloud Connector
  • Configure Access Control (HTTP)
  • How to Use a Cloud to On-Premise Scenario

6. Observability and Monitoring

Unit 6 imparts the observability strategy of SAP BTP with SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) in its core. It offers central observability tools like business process, integration, user, or health monitoring. Including the local observability tools SAP Alert Notification Service and SAP Automation Pilot, SAP BTP offers a comprehensive monitoring approach.

There is no exercise in this unit. If you want to know more, please check our learning journey Operating with SAP Cloud ALM, especially unit 2 (Using Monitoring and Analysis).

7. Cost and Usage Management

Unit 7 closes the learning journey with cost and usage management, a topic often requested by customers.

There is no exercise in this unit but many slides and videos with hints around handling the costs of SAP BTP efficiently.

 

Summary

If you have read so far, it seems that you are really thinking about consuming the learning journey. Don’t hesitate and jump right in:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/administrating-sap-business-technology-platform

If you want to put your hands on some live system, the SAP BTP trial is just on click away:

https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/pricing.html

If you are more interested in a customer course for SAP BTP administration, please visit https://training.sap.com. The ADMCLD (Introduction to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Administration) is a 3-day-course offered as physical and virtual event:

https://training.sap.com/course/admcld

For any other questions around the offerings of SAP Learning Services, please jump to our community page and get in contact with us:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/training-certification

Happy learning
Stefan

entry.title: Re: In Subscription Billing, for monthly recurring charge...
entry.content:
Hi @mnic_17
In general, it's also possible to define everything in one rate and make use of the conditional operators in your pricing element specfications.
You need an additional field/information indicating which rate to apply of course. 
And then e.g. use the string comparison.
https://help.sap.com/docs/PRICE_CALCULATION/53987d38467243e28cbeb8514d015666/1cb96febff5543b7a9087efa90c4518a.html

Best regards
Stefan

regards,

Asim Zaidi

entry.title: LIMITATION OF FORM TEMPLATE CUSTOMIZATION
entry.content:
Hi All,

Good Day!

Do the customizations for the pre-delivered templates in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public, have limitations? Like number of fields to be added, the tables, data, object, etc. 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – How to fix Changed and Outdated Catalogs
entry.content: Hi @MariePerdon  Can you clarify what you are seeing? Or perhaps add a screenshot? There are no user-specific catalogs. There are only client-specific and cross-client. Outdated and Changed only apply to client-specific catalogs.

entry.title: Datasphere source system assignment pointing to Dev after moving to Qual system for replication flow
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have a situation where our replications flows are pointing to Dev systems after moving to quality systems.

with on prem we have BDLS which take cares of Mapping but in cloud solution what is the approach? 

anyone have faced similar kind of issues?

Regards,

Raj

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

Thank you for providing us this digital skills initiative. I have been self-learning SAP SD for almost a year now and I'm amazed of how big the SAP world is. As a female college student who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt, i would love to have a free attempt to take the exam and upskill myself with an SAP certificate. 

Thank you and Best Regards

Anh

entry.title: Re: How to maintain Priority in Purchase requisition and...
entry.content:
Hi,

Refer to the KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0003210447

You would like to set different work item priorities based on your own custom logic for Procurement documents.
For this purpose you can use the BADI MMPUR_WFL_CONTEXT_ENHANCE.

For Purchase Requisitions you are able to set the work item priority but for Purchase Orders the work item priorities cannot be set. 

The BADI MMPUR_WFL_CONTEXT_ENHANCE is only fully enabled for Purchase Requisitions.
Setting a work item priority for Purchase Orders via the BADI is not yet enabled.  

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: SAP UI5 API Calls - No Preview Available
entry.content:
Hello All, 

Facing an issue with Microsoft Edge since yesterday, where i am unable to see the Preview/Response in the Network Tab under Developer Tools. All the Get/Post API calls, say No Preview Available. Is anyone else also facing this? Anyone who has some pointers to fix this?

SAPUI5

entry.title: Re: Employee Central - Compensation Promotion integration in Successfactors

Hello  @xavierlegarrec 

we are facing a new issue with the Promotion Standard funcitonality since yesterday.

When we tried to insert a new job classification through the promotion link, after we pressed on Save, the system gave us this pop-up error: 

 

Next, we tried to reopen the form, bot the promotion link dissappeared. 

Can you please give us any ideas on how to examine this further?

Thank you in advance for your valuable time.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Central Business Configuration 2409
entry.content:
With the latest release for SAP Central Business Configuration, exciting new features are available. This blog post will provide you with a comprehensive overview of these features.

Improved Configuration Activities Filtering Based on Country Relevance

In the Configure Your Business Processes app, a new filter allows you to screen configuration activities based on their significance for specific countries or regions in scope. For example, it’s now possible to filter configuration activities that are relevant to one or multiple countries/regions in scope or globally. This functionality allows you to quickly identify the right configuration activities, thereby saving you valuable time.

Screenshot of the Country/Region filter in the Configure Your Business Processes app

Enhanced User Experience Within Change Logs

In the Change Logs app, we’ve enhanced the search functionality: The Changed By column now includes a filter where you can enter a username. This enhancement helps you quickly pinpoint changes made by a particular user, thereby saving you valuable time. Note that case sensitivity is not an issue as the search is not case-sensitive.

Screenshot of the Changed By column filter in the Change Logs app

For a quick summary, check out this video about What's New in 2409. Don’t forget to also use the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal, which provides further details and links about all the recent changes.

That wraps up the new features this time. Make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have an idea on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the SAP Customer Influence portal to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano,

My name is Sameer, and I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude to SAP for this incredible initiative.

I am eager to contribute and be part of Digital Skills Initiative, with a particular interest in SAP Fiori. Unfortunately, I had to take a temporary break from my career to care for my parents, but I am now ready to restart my journey as an SAP developer.

With prior experience in the manufacturing industry, I have developed a strong foundation in Java, JavaScript, and programming concepts such as OOPS and RESTful APIs. I am excited to apply these skills to SAP Fiori and make a meaningful impact.

As someone who identifies as a first-generation college graduate and lacks the financial means for certification attempts, I would greatly appreciate any advice or opportunities that could help me move forward in this field.

Best regards,
Sameer

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024

entry.title: Re: Changing Fixed Asset in S4HANA Cloud via API
entry.content: Hi, soap envelope gives the error. This is my payload: <soapenv:Envelope xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/" xmlns:fi="http://sap.com/xi/FI-AA" xmlns:yy1="http://SAPCustomFields.com/YY1_RevaluationOffAsse"> <soapenv:Header/> <soapenv:Body> <fi:FixedAssetChangeBulkRequestMessage> <MessageHeader> <ID>MSG_2020-06-03_01</ID> <CreationDateTime>2024-10-01T00:00:00Z</CreationDateTime> </MessageHeader> <FixedAssetChangeRequest> <MessageHeader> <ID>MSG_2020-06-03_01</ID> <CreationDateTime>2024-10-01T00:00:00Z</CreationDateTime> </MessageHeader> <FixedAsset> <CompanyCode>1000</CompanyCode> <MasterFixedAsset>30000021</MasterFixedAsset> <FixedAsset>0</FixedAsset> <General> <FixedAssetDescription>Installations/Fixtures/Fittings</FixedAssetDescription> <AssetAdditionalDescription>string</AssetAdditionalDescription> <AssetSerialNumber>string</AssetSerialNumber> <BaseUnit>s</BaseUnit> </General> <Inventory> <InventoryNumber>string</InventoryNumber> <LastInventoryDate>2024-09-20</LastInventoryDate> <InventoryNote>string</InventoryNote> </Inventory> <AccountAssignment actionCode="01"> <CostCenter>1000TEST</CostCenter> <ProfitCenter>1000TEST</ProfitCenter> <Room>s</Room> <Plant>s</Plant> <AssetLocation>s</AssetLocation> <VehicleLicensePlateNumber>s</VehicleLicensePlateNumber> </AccountAssignment> </FixedAsset> </FixedAssetChangeRequest> </fi:FixedAssetChangeBulkRequestMessage> </soapenv:Body> </soapenv:Envelope>

Br
Martin

entry.title: Sales Office scenarios in Two-Tier ERP
entry.content:
Reflecting on the recently concluded Two-Tier ERP Confluence event, where we focused on integration and various sales office scenarios, this blog will bring a business perspective to the following key sales office scenarios and their significance:

  • Sale from Local Sales Office (Goods and Basic Services)
  • Sale from Central Stock, Direct Delivery to Customer from HQ
  • Returns Handling at Sales Office, with Credit and Debit Memo
  • Sale from HQ, Direct Procurement from Local Sales Office
  • Services from Sales Office for HQ
  • Installation, Warranty, Repair from Sales Office
  • Predictive Maintenance with AIN from Sales Office

Now, let’s take a deeper look at the different business scenarios, particularly focusing on sales from local offices.

A local sales office scenario is a prime example of loosely coupled scenario, which has been highlighted in the Two-Tier ERP Overview. In contrast, other sales office cases often involve dependent subsidiaries, which can be referred as tightly coupled scenario. You can find the relevant info here in the Two-Tier ERP Overview. Regardless of the structural differences between loosely coupled and tightly coupled, most of these scenarios can be grouped into two main categories:

  1. Inventory Sales and Returns Management
  2. Sale of services

Among the scenarios, listed above, the sale from the local sales office is well-known, but understanding why this scenario is crucial from a business perspective requires a deeper dive. This scenario enables regional sales offices to operate autonomously, driving localized decision-making, faster customer response times, and greater flexibility in meeting market demands. It is particularly beneficial when dealing with different compliance requirements, currency, or language variations across regions.

The Drop-Ship Scenario: An End-to-End Perspective

Let's now turn our attention to one of the most common sales processes from End-to-End perspective is “sale from central stock with direct delivery to the customer from headquarters”, commonly known as Drop-Ship scenario. This process offers significant advantages in maintaining lean inventories at sales offices while ensuring customer satisfaction with direct deliveries.

The following is an End-to-End scenario that offers detailed process information, outlining where each step is executed within a business context.

Future Exploration

In the upcoming blogs, we will take a deeper dive into each of the process steps outlined in the End-to-End scenario, exploring different variants and how they can benefit the business.

We will also take a deeper look in to Returns Management, in future blogs.

Sale of Services in a Two-Tier ERP Deployment

As outlined earlier, in Fig 1, the sale of services is another key scenario. For the sale of services, as outlined above, local subsidiaries manage both customer interaction and service delivery, allowing for greater agility and a better understanding of local needs. The Two-Tier ERP deployment model enables seamless integration between the local and HQ systems, ensuring smooth data flow across the organization.

For more information on warranty management, you can refer the blog here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/warranty-claim-process-in-headquarter-subsidiary-model-for-two-tier-erp/ba-p/13743032

You can also reach out two-tier ERP community for more clarifications

entry.title: Re: Business objects BUS2032
entry.content:
Hi,

you will know when you see it 😉

In general you do not need it, but some customizing for example OAC3 is based on the object type.

Also workflow leverage the business object.

 

Kind regards

Robert

entry.title: Re: Integrate My Inbox link to SAP Flexible Workflow E-Mail notification
entry.content:
Thank you very much for your help earlier.

Im trying to extend CDS view C_CRDTMEMOREQWRKFLWEMLTEMPLATE which is for email template SD_SLS_CMR_APPROVAL_EMAIL
for notifying approvers about new credit memo requests that require approval.

The clients want system id to be included in the header of the email.
I see theres no session variables for system ID.
How do I extend the CDS view C_CRDTMEMOREQWRKFLWEMLTEMPLATE to get the system ID?

entry.title: Teams Management, Data Privileges and Data Locks in SAP PaPM Cloud - Universal Model
entry.content:
Hi everyone! Welcome to my blogpost for Teams Management, Data Locks, and Data Privileges in SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Universal Model (SAP PaPM Cloud - UM).

Before I begin, just wanted to ask if you are now familiar with roles assignment for you to have access in certain applications? If not, I suggest that you check and go through the Roles overview in SAP PaPM Cloud - Universal Model blogpost as it provides important user specific role assignment before going to the Universal Model application. From that blogpost, it was explained how specific role template can be assigned to users to limit their system access. On top of that, it was also discussed that with the help of teams, some application objects such as environment can be further restricted by a specific team as well as the data coming from the Model Entity application. This will be helpful as we go along with this blogpost.

As these topics were covered from that blogpost, let me focus in my blogpost on the following topics about teams and how to apply it in the SAP PaPM Cloud – Universal Model applications.

A. What is teams management?
a.1 Creation of teams in the application.
a.2 Creation of user attribute in BTP.

B. Providing privileges and assigning restrictions through the following:
b.1 Application object control through Reader Team and Writer Team.
b.2 Information or data control through Data Privileges.
b.3 Information of data control through Data Locks.
b.4 Additional information: Combination of Data Privileges and Data Locks.

A. What is Teams Management?

Let me talk about what teams management is.

Teams Management provides additional security and control with the help of applications and information. These teams are groups of users that work together in environments, data, processes, activities, and reports. User can only see the activities that are assigned to their team.

Examples:

  • Modeler Team A can only see the environment they are modeling or part of, and Modeler Team B can only see their environment.
  • Team Philippines can only see data coming from the Philippines, and Team Germany can only see data coming from Germany.

I will discuss further on how to create teams in the application and where you can maintain the created teams.

a.1 Creation of teams in the application
Here’s how you can create a team in the SAP PaPM Cloud - Universal Model application:

i.  Go to Universal Model application and select Manage Teams from the Process Application.

ii. As an example, I have created two teams in the Manage Teams application.

  • Team: DEMOREAD
  • Team: DEMOWRITE

iii. Upon creation, you will notice that Authorizations section is empty, this is expected since there is no user assigned yet to the team.

For more information about Teams creation, see Manage Teams.

a.2 Creation of user attribute in BTP

The creation of roles for a specific team in BTP is extremely important because you will be able to increase security by limiting the user’s access in the applications.

As there are no user assigned to the team, I will demonstrate on how you can create a user and how it will be mapped in the Manage Teams application.

i. You can access the BTP cockpit via this link - SAP BTP Cockpit

ii. Once you are already in the BTP cockpit, choose the tenant where you want to create the team and follow the steps below on how you can create Role and Role Collections.

  • Role creation:
    Take note of the team you created in Manage Teams application as this is to be used as value in BTP to have it mapped successfully.
    For more information about Role creation, see Build User Attribute Roles
  • Role Collections creation:
    Note: You cannot assign a role directly to a user instead you have to first include the role in the Role Collection and assign the created Role Collection to a user.
    For more information about Role Collection creation, see Build Role Collections

iii. After the successful creation of the Role and Role Collections via SAP BTP cockpit, the team and the user added is now displayed and mapped under Authorizations of Manage Teams application.

For more information about assigning role and role collections in BTP, see User Management

Note:
The user will only be added to the list upon first login and visiting an application they have access to if the administrator cannot see the assigned user to the team. This is a security feature of the Manage Teams application.

Now that we have completed the process on how to setup teams, let us see where we can use them, how to grant privileges, and assign restriction in the data records as well as access limitation with the help of teams through the SAP PaPM Cloud – Universal Model applications.

B. Providing privileges and assigning restrictions through the following:

b.1 Application object control through Reader Team and Writer Team

Are you wondering how you can limit the users access in the application? How can read and write team affect a certain object and data? If you are interested in knowing the answers to these, you should continue with this blogpost.

To answer the questions, you can limit the users access in the application by assigning restrictions through Reader Team and Writer Team. You will find in the table an overview of expected behavior once users are assigned in teams in the application.  

 

Below is an example scenario wherein a power user such as MODEL_ALL can see and edit all 3 environments such as CAMPX, CAMPY and CAMPZ. Now let’s focus on environment specifics: 

  • CAMPX can only be seen or edited by TEAMA.
  • CAMPY can only be seen or edited by TEAMB.
  • CAMPZ environment can only be seen and edited by the environment owner (environment creator) since it is not assigned with any teams. 

USERS ASSIGNED TO MODEL_ALL ROLE (ENVIRONMENT ACCESS)

TEAM A (ENVIRONMENT ACCESS)
Note:
These are users without MODEL_ALL role

TEAM B (ENVIRONMENT ACCESS)
Note:
These are users without MODEL_ALL role

CAMP Z (ENVIRONMENT ACCESS): Environment Owner (Environment Creator)  
Note:
These are users without MODEL_ALL role

We basically covered where teams can be used in the application. The usual question is: What exactly teams do and how it will affect the data records of the activity?

Let me discuss more about what teams can do, how to grant privileges, and assign restriction in the data records as well as access limitation through the following SAP PaPM Cloud – Universal Model applications:

b.2 Information or data control through Data Privileges 

To give you an overview of Data Privileges, it is an application that provides additional data protection and control of which data can a user see within the set of records. It allows the process owner to grant an authorization for certain users to have read and write access to the data records. If the users have read access, they will have the visibility to see the sets of records; then, if the users have the write access, they will be able to maintain and manipulate data records linked to a certain field and optionally to a function.

Note:
It is essential that a team is assigned for the data privileges to work.

Example:
If you are the process owner and you wanted to provide set of users who can only see specific data records, how can you do it in the application?

We will use this data records to showcase how data privileges work.

I will give you 2 different scenarios on how you can use the Data Privileges in the application with the help of teams created in the in the application.

Scenario 1: Reader Team Access

From the sample data records (above), the process owner will assign reader team to only have display access from the specific data records. We will use the Data Privileges application to be able to do that. See below configuration in the system:

  • Data privileges creation:
  1. Go to Manage Data Privileges application and choose Create.
  2. Provide the Runtime Environment to be used.
  3. Choose Continue.
  4. Choose an activity where configurations in data privilege shall be applied.
  5. Choose a function where the source of data record shall be applied for data privilege.
  6. Select the team you want to give access.
  7. Choose Create.
  • These are the objects used in data privileges creation:

We will use the fields and selections from the table below that the reader team will only have display access to:

As we have successfully created a data privileges entry for the reader team, I will show how data privileges affects data manipulation in the Manage Activities application for reader team.

It was expected that reader team can only have display access in the data records of the company PROJECTEK CORP but any modifications in the data records was not permitted and healthy error messages were issued by the application.

Scenario 2: Writer Team Access

At this point, you might be curious how the data privileges work when the process owner give the writer team the access to edit the activity data records. Assigning of writer team in data privileges has the same step as we did in the reader team.

We will use the fields and selections from the table below that the writer team will have edit access to:

After I have assigned a writer team and maintained the same fields and selections, let us try to see on what users can see and do in the data records of Manage Activities application.

You must take note that all users assigned in write access should have the read access too, to have the possibility to see the activity and data records.

Let us proceed to the Manage Activities application where the defined fields and selections in data privileges can be used.  

As you have seen, the writer team was able to manipulate the data records of company PROJECTSY GROUP as expected.

Since I already provided you two simple scenarios in this blogpost wherein, I explained the relevance of the reader team and writer team from data privileges perspective. I hope it provides enough information on how you can control and restrict your data records effectively. Here’s a short summary of the scenarios I discussed:

  • Users that are part of the reader team maintained in Data Privileges application will only have read access and cannot manipulate the data records.
  • If both users of writer team and reader team have been assigned in the Data Privileges application, only the users from writer team can manipulate the data records of the activity while users of reader team will only have read access.

Following this blogpost, I will teach you more on how you can provide a higher level of restriction on all exposed analytic and transactional data records using Manage Data Locks application in SAP PaPM Cloud – Universal Model.

b.3 Information or data control through Data Locks 

Now you know that the data privilege grants access to see and edit specific data records by the reader team and writer team, our next topic will be assigning data locks to restrict updating the specific data records on top of the selection criteria given by the process owner to the writer team. 

Before we proceed, let me define what Data Locks is. It is an application in Universal Model that gives extra layer of protection to limit the editing access to the set of data records. It gives you the authority to lock the data to the assigned writer team users and restrict their access to edit and update the data records. You can set up data locking and specify areas of function and activity to lock, restrict, and unlock.

I will give an overview of what data locking can do to prevent changes to a specific data record.

Example:

If you are the process owner and you wanted to lock and restrict specific data records, how can you do it in the application?

We will use this data records to showcase how data locks work.

See below configuration oh how to enable data locks in the application:

  • Data Locks creation:
  1. Go to Manage Data Locks application and choose Create.
  2. Provide the Runtime Environment to be used.
  3. Choose Continue.
  4. Choose an activity where the configurations in data locks shall be applied.
  5. Choose a function where the source of the data record shall be applied for data locking.
  6. Select the team that is not allowed to perform configurations and data manipulation.
  7. Choose Create.
  • These are the objects used in data locks creation:

We will use the fields and selections from the table below to restrict access by the writer team:

Now let us proceed to the Manage Activities application where the defined fields and selections in data locks can be used.  

You must take note as well that all users assigned in write access should have the read access too, to have the possibility to see the activity and data records.

As a result, the writer team can see all the data records but limited to updating Year 2024 since the process owner locked the Year 2022-2023. Attempting to update the data records of Year 2022-2023 will trigger a healthy error message “FORBIDDEN”.

b.4 Additional Information: Combination of Data Privileges and Data Locks 

Now that we have covered on how giving privileges in the specific data records and assigning data restrictions via data locks application works, do you want to see how the application behaves if we combine giving privileges and data locking for writer team?

Example:

The process owner gave data privileges to two writer teams respectively Team A and Team B wherein both teams can only view and edit companies that they are assigned to.  With the given data privileges, the process owner will also be restricting data records modification for writer teams on certain years by assigning data locks.

We will use the fields and selections from the table below for Team A:

We will use the fields and selections from the table below for Team B:

Now let us see the results for both teams when the writer users try to access the data records with the given privileges and restrictions.

In summary, once the process owner maintains the user as part of the writer team in the Data Locks application and assigned fields and selection in a data, it will override the rights of the writer team users given in the Data Privileges application to perform data record manipulation even these users have the write access in the application.

Here are the following results of giving privileges and locking data in the same activity data records.

Now that you already know how you can limit the users access in the application and how reader and writer teams affect a certain object and data, you can start configuring your teams, data privileges and data locks in SAP PaPM Cloud – Universal Model.

If you want to learn more about other applications in SAP PaPM Cloud – Universal Model that can be used as information function and data enrichment function, follow, and track our upcoming blogposts for Collection and Model Collection functions.

I hope you learned and enjoyed reading this as much as I enjoy preparing this blogpost for all of you. Should you have any inputs, questions, and suggestions you can add a comment below 😊

entry.title: How to add custom property to FLP (Cloud Portal Service)
entry.content:
We have a use case where the same UI5 apps are to be integrated in various FLPs (each belonging to a different tenant). The app logic is mostly the same for each tenant, but there are some slight custom logic for some tenants.

Is there a way we could get the tenants to add an identifier to their FLP, so that in our UI5 apps, we are able to get this property and use it to run our tenant-specific logic?

BR,

Spyros

entry.title: Re: Need Help Identifying the Best Processing Instruction...

entry.title: How to list users group with sap bo rest api?
entry.content:
Hi,

I want to get usergroups of a user. I am trying v1/users/{userId}/userGroups but it return 404. There are couple of endpoints like v1/userGroups/<usergroup_id>/users but it return users in a userGroup. I need to get user groups of a user. How can I achieve this using rest api?

entry.title: Create Modify or delete role objects using BApi
entry.content:
Hi,

I need to know if there is any way to modify create or add objects to roles. I am seeing that the only standard thing is to use the /GRCPI/GRIA_MASS_UPD_ROLE function , I also use other functions to achieve my purpose such as PRGN_AUTO_GENERATE_PROFILE_NEW and i use the RFC_READ_TABLE function to read from the AGR_DEFINE table and there if a role is a parent or child. At the end I commit the changes with the bapi. But I can't get it to change or add any object to the role.

Regards

entry.title: Re: How to consume S/4HANA Cloud API using ABAP program f...
entry.content:
Hi @Gheorghe-Valer 

yes, for local write access, please consume released BO interfaces as described in Business Object Consumption. If a released BO interfaces is not available (yet), but a corresponding API service is available, then you might consider implementing a remote write access using the API service as temporary mitigation. However, please keep in mind that this comes with several drawbacks compared to the usage of a BO interfaces that you need to consider. For example, a remote call issues an implicit database commit (see Database Commit), has its own LUW (transactional consistency), and causes additional overhead (performance). Thus, this approach is generally not recommended and should be used as mitigation only.

Best regards
Kai

entry.title: Re: Guide to use HANA NSE in the BW/4HANA system
entry.content:
Note that manual changes to tables generated by SAP BW or SAP BW/4HANA, as suggested in this blog, are not supported.

2259872 - Changes to generated BW tables with database tools 

Regards,
Dominik

entry.title: Optimizing HR with SAP Signavio and SuccessFactors: Strategic Business Modeling for Enhanced Process
entry.content:
The HR landscape is undergoing rapid evolution, prompting organizations to seek innovative solutions for optimizing processes and aligning them with overarching business strategies. SAP Signavio has emerged as a powerful tool, enabling organizations to not only manage but also transform their HR processes in conjunction with SAP SuccessFactors. This blog explores how SAP Signavio integrates with SuccessFactors to offer a comprehensive approach to business process management and transformation within the HR domain.

1. The Need for Transformation in HR

HR functions have traditionally been seen as administrative and transactional. However, modern organizations recognize the strategic value of HR in driving business success. This shift necessitates innovative solutions that can streamline HR processes, enhance efficiency, and ensure alignment with business goals. SAP Signavio, in conjunction with SAP SuccessFactors, provides the tools needed to achieve these objectives.

2. SAP Signavio: A Quick Overview

SAP Signavio is a comprehensive suite designed for business process management and transformation. It enables organizations to document, model, analyze, and optimize their processes. When integrated with SAP SuccessFactors, it offers a holistic approach to managing HR processes, from recruitment and onboarding to performance management and employee engagement.

SAP Signavio's capabilities include:

  • Process Modeling: Visualize and document business processes.
  • Process Mining: Analyze real-time data to identify bottlenecks and inefficiencies.
  • Collaboration: Facilitate communication between stakeholders for continuous improvement.
  • Process Optimization: Implement process changes and track performance metrics.

3. The Intersection of SAP Signavio and SuccessFactors

In the dynamic world of Human Resources (HR), the integration of SAP Signavio with SAP SuccessFactors marks a significant leap forward in optimizing and transforming HR processes. This intersection brings together the strengths of both platforms to deliver a comprehensive solution for strategic business modeling and efficient HR management.

SAP SuccessFactors utilizes SAP Signavio Process Manager to document its industry-leading processes, all available in the SAP SuccessFactors process library. This library is accessible to SAP SuccessFactors partners and customers through the SAP Signavio Process Collaboration Hub in the SAP Signavio Process Explorer

3.1. End-to-End Process Visibility

With SAP Signavio, HR teams can visualize and document end-to-end processes in SuccessFactors, such as the recruitment journey, performance management, and employee engagement initiatives. This visibility allows HR professionals to identify inefficiencies and standardize processes across different business units.

The leading practice process diagrams in the SAP SuccessFactors process library are built with the goal of defining end-to-end HR processes specifically for SAP SuccessFactors solutions. They include all relevant activities in swim lanes, which represent the major user roles.

4. Use Cases: How Organizations Benefit from the Integration

4.1. Optimizing the Recruitment Process

A multinational company was struggling with lengthy and inconsistent recruitment processes across its regional offices. By integrating SAP Signavio with SuccessFactors Recruitment, they were able to map the entire recruitment process, identify bottlenecks, and implement standardized workflows. This resulted in a 30% reduction in time-to-hire and improved candidate experience.

4.2. Improving Employee Retention

A financial services firm used SAP Signavio to analyze data from SuccessFactors Employee Central and Performance & Goals. By mapping and evaluating the performance review process, they discovered inconsistencies that were impacting employee satisfaction and retention. With this insight, they redesigned the process to include more regular feedback and development discussions, leading to a 15% decrease in employee turnover.

4.3. Enhancing Learning and Development

An organization in the healthcare sector used SAP Signavio to streamline their learning and development processes managed through SuccessFactors Learning. By optimizing the training program design and delivery processes, they improved training completion rates and reduced administrative overhead by 20%.

5. Best Practices for Implementing SAP Signavio with SuccessFactors

5.1. Align HR Processes with Business Strategy

Before integrating SAP Signavio with SuccessFactors, ensure that your HR processes are aligned with the organization's strategic objectives. This alignment will help in identifying key areas for improvement and drive meaningful changes.

5.2. Engage Stakeholders Early

Successful process transformation requires buy-in from all stakeholders. Engage HR leaders, IT teams, and business managers early in the process to ensure everyone’s needs and concerns are addressed.

5.3. Leverage Process Mining for Insights

Use SAP Signavio’s process mining capabilities to gain insights into existing HR processes before making any changes. This data-driven approach will help in identifying real issues and measuring the impact of improvements.

5.4. Foster a Culture of Continuous Improvement

Encourage your HR team to regularly review and update processes using SAP Signavio. Continuous improvement should be a core part of your HR strategy to keep pace with changing business needs.

5.5. Invest in Training and Change Management

Implementing new tools and processes can be challenging. Invest in training and change management to ensure your HR team is equipped to use SAP Signavio effectively and embrace new ways of working.

6. Conclusion

The integration of SAP Signavio with SuccessFactors represents a significant opportunity for organizations to transform their HR processes. By leveraging the powerful process modeling, mining, and optimization capabilities of SAP Signavio, HR leaders can drive efficiency, ensure compliance, and align HR strategies with broader business objectives. As organizations continue to evolve, those that embrace this integration will be well-positioned to adapt and thrive in a dynamic business environment.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano, I am Osama and I fall in the categories listed below:

  • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
  • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
  • First generation college students
  • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

As an aspiring SAP SuccessFactors consultant, I am passionate about leveraging technology to drive organizational success and enhance employee experiences. With a strong foundation in HR principles and a keen interest in cloud-based solutions, I aim to help organizations optimize their human capital management processes. I am committed to continuous learning and eager to apply my skills in system configuration, implementation, and data analysis to support businesses in achieving their strategic goals. My goal is to bridge the gap between technology and human resources, ensuring that companies can thrive in today’s dynamic work environment.

entry.title: SAP PE Session "Future Data Warehouse – Datasphere, SAC etc." (Virtual, October 10, 2024)
entry.content:
Virtual

October 10, 2024

Agenda

Dear Valued Customer,

We are pleased to invite you to the next SAP Premium Engagement Session focusing on the crucial topic “Future Data Warehouse – Datasphere, SAC etc.”. This event is taking place virtually on October 10th, 2024. Please find the detailed agenda here.

In this event our experts will share the overall view of SAP Data Analytics and Future Data Warehousing, as well as dig into valuable insights about how Premium Engagement can help you in the journey of data and analytics. We will also share customer cases and the tools and processes.

This is an excellent opportunity to gain actionable knowledge and network with like-minded professionals who are embarking on a similar journey towards digital transformation. We invite you to join us and participate in this engaging discussion.

Our Workshops

These sessions and workshop series focus on a specific topic and the purpose is to enable our customers to understand the product portfolio and innovative technologies of SAP in order to drive adoption and consumption. As specifically now, it is more important than ever to help companies to be successful and to respond to changing markets by transforming into intelligent enterprises in the experience economy.

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

entry.title: Re: Business objects BUS2032
entry.content:
Hello @Utkarsh30 

Business Objects like BUS2023 are used in several functionalities predominantly in workflow scenarios, but also in ALE integrations and ArchiveLink (attachment storage).

See Business Object Builder to learn more about business objects and Tutorial: Workflow Programming if you are interested in implementing or enhancing business objects. 

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: SAP MaxAttention IWS "Business Process Intelligence with SAP Signavio" (October 17, 2024)
entry.content:
Virtual

October 17, 2024

Agenda

Dear Valued Customer,

We are pleased to invite you to the SAP MaxAttention Innovation Workshop focusing on ‘Business Process Intelligence with SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite’ virtually taking place on October 17, 2024. Please find the current agenda here.

SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite allows organizations to achieve their maximum potential through continuous process transformation and improvement. It helps companies make the intelligent enterprise a reality: understand the operations instantly and holistically, react, and fix them immediately.

SAP® MaxAttention™ Innovation Workshop is designed to help you start the Business Process Intelligence journey using SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite. This workshop will focus on value realization across organizations using SAP Signavio Process Insights, Process Manager and Process Intelligence tools also learn SAP Signavio journey with SAP Max Attention.

Our Workshops

These sessions and workshop series focus on a specific topic and the purpose is to enable our customers to understand the product portfolio and innovative technologies of SAP in order to drive adoption and consumption. As specifically now, it is more important than ever to help companies to be successful and to respond to changing markets by transforming into intelligent enterprises in the experience economy.

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

I am now keen to pursue the certification itself, as I believe it will enhance my skills and career prospects in the field of data analytics.

I would appreciate any guidance on the certification process and available resources along with a voucher code if applicable as it will help me financially.

@David_Chaviano

entry.title: Work Zone My Inbox / Task Center: solution to get Open Task to generate a URL accessible through WZ
entry.content:
When the user navigates to "Open Task" and the task is configured to show details in a Web Dynpro ABAP application then the backend system generates a URL pointing to that backend system directly. For a seamless experience, it not only requires that backend to be exposed on the internet (with or without a gateway system), but also requires SAML2 to be setup. It defeats the purpose of Work Zone being the internet facing portal making backend applications accessible via the SAP Cloud Connector.

Is there a solution offered by SAP that allows the Open Task action to lead to a URL that is accessible within Work Zone itself?

Similar question here from a while back, but in the context of the Fiori Launchpad service with no solution: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/fiori-inbox-cloud-default-webgui-wda/qaq-p/12514253

I hope it's supported. If it's not, then I plan to explore enhancing the class that generates the URL and return a link to WZ targetting the Shell-startWDA intent.

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 API Calls - No Preview Available

entry.title: Re: SAC Add-in for Powerpoint
entry.content: Is this currently only in power point web? When is the next release coming for power point desktop?

entry.title: CDS Views to Fetch Revision level of Material?
entry.content:
Need your help in finding the CDS views to fetch revision level of a material. We have couple of forms like Goods receipt label, PO form etc. where client wants to print revision level of material.

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CDS Views to Fetch Revision level of Material?
entry.content:
The CDS view I_PRODUCT contains the field MaterialRevisionLevel for retrieving the Revision Level of a Material

 

https://api.sap.com/cdsviews/I_PRODUCT

entry.title: Re: #COE#BUMA Using Teams and Responsibility on both PR a...
entry.content:
Hi @_JohnL_ ,

Creating a different responsibility role for each PR approval team and PO approval team is an easy approach and we can create different Teams for different org structures like for each Company code etc...

We can still change the Responsibility rules, Responsibility contexts and team categories which can be used so that we can use the same team for both the PR and PO approval if we create custom Responsibility context or we can use the BADI.
Check the below SAP help port on Extensibility for Responsible Management.
 https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/8308e6d301d54584a33cd04a9861bc52/be4c7a10997646cea7cecccf891c98f8.html

I am still not aware why you are going to use the same team for both PR and PO approval.

Hope this helps!!!!

Thanks and Regards,
Suryaprakash.

entry.title: Chrome v129 is bugged with $batch odata calls. Preview / response does not work
entry.content:
Recently I updated Chrome to V129 and saw that all my $batch odata calls were empty in Preview / Response tab. I googled around and discovered it is a bug in Chrome (also Canary current version). It is listed and will be picked up I think 🙂

In the meantime you can use the UI5 inspector oData Request tab (I was not aware of this tab, thanks Robin van het Hof).

Hope this helps.

Noël

entry.title: Re: Chrome v129 is bugged with $batch odata calls. Preview / response does not work

entry.title: Fiori WebGUI/WebDynpro/URL tile opens in the incorrect language
entry.content:
Hi All,

If you encounter such a problem, remove the language value in the rfc destination in sm59 as in the note.

https://me.sap.com/notes/0002742144

entry.title: SAP Business AI Testing = SAP API Testing
entry.content:
Introduction - why testing APIs is important for SAP Business AI 

When we talk about SAP Business AI in business we need to remember one thing: AI cannot work without APIs. In fact, testing SAP Business AI is all about testing how well APIs work. Let’s break down why this is so important.

Figure 1 - generative AI cannot do any business activity without SAP APIs 

Why APIs Are Important for SAP Business AI

APIs (Application Programming Interfaces) are like bridges that let different programs talk to each other. AI, especially advanced AI like generative AI, needs APIs to get information and connect with other systems. Without these connections, AI can't do much.

Integration is the backbone of digital transformations, and APIs are the building blocks for every SAP customer who sells, buys, or transports their products. This means APIs are the key to making all of a company’s systems work together. For AI, APIs are what allow it to be part of that big picture.

How APIs Help SAP Business AI Work

Think of AI like a chef who needs ingredients to make a meal. The API is the helper that brings those ingredients to the kitchen. Without it, the chef (AI) can’t do their job. Here’s how APIs help AI:

  1. Getting Data: AI needs a lot of information to make smart decisions. APIs bring that data from different places to the AI.
  2. Executing Business Activities: To do any business task—like creating an order, delivering goods, or managing inventory—AI needs to use APIs to make things happen. For example, if AI is asked to create a new sales order, it has to send a request through an API to the system that manages orders. Without APIs, AI wouldn’t be able to carry out any business activities.
  3. Building New Solutions: APIs allow businesses to create new smart tools by connecting AI with existing software, kind of like mixing different ingredients to create a new recipe.

Figure 2 - SAP API Testing with Int4 Suite = SAP Business AI testing 

How does SAP Business AI execute business activities on SAP S/4HANA

SAP has many ways to expose it’s SAP S/4HANA and their use depends on the context:

  1. SAP APIs (Webservices, ODATA, etc.) can be exposed directly from SAP S/4HANA. Some typical ways include generating web services or ODATA interfaces.
  2. In order to manage APIs exposed by SAP S/4HANA some other components might be used like SAP BTP API Management (part of SAP Integration Suite) and those APIs can also be exposed by SAP Business Accelerator Hub – https://api.sap.com
  3. In most cases however SAP APIs are build and designed using SAP Integration Suite. SAP Integration Suite is the go to solution for building the SAP S/4HANA Clean Core side by side extensions which include building and orchestrating complex integration scenarios and exposing them to the outside world (to SAP Business AI, Joule, Copilots, etc.)

Why Testing SAP Business AI = Testing SAP APIs

Since APIs are so important to AI, testing SAP Business AI is really about testing how well the APIs work. If the APIs don’t work right, the AI won’t either. Here are some key things to check when testing APIs:

  1. Getting and Posting the Right Data: Making sure the API sends the right information to the AI, so it can make the best decisions and making sure that APIs exposed to AI are working as they we designed
  2. Working Together: Testing if AI and other SAP systems can communicate smoothly through APIs.
  3. Speed and Performance: Ensuring the APIs work quickly and don’t slow down the AI’s responses.

In short, AI can’t exist without APIs. And in SAP systems, this connection is even more important because everything is linked together through APIs. So, if you're testing SAP Business AI, you're really testing the APIs that make it all possible.

Further Reference

Learn how to test SAP APIs with Int4 Suite - Avoid SAP S/4HANA Project Delays with Third-Party Systems Service Virtualization

entry.title: Re: DUMP MESSAGE_TYPE_X Program C_OI_ERRORS===================CP component BC-FES-OFF
entry.content: solutio of this error in my report --> go to option inside the report and select in section office integration type of output inactive and put flag continue and try again to excecuted the transaction. in my case this has resolve the issues.

entry.title: Re: How to add custom property to FLP (Cloud Portal Service)
entry.content:
You have some possibilities:

  • using a parameter in your tile configuration
  • reading out the url
  • a CAP service that provides you the tenant information
  • a CAP service with feature toggles

I would recommend the last two options in case this is a multitenant application. I also recommend to put the tenant specific login in the CAP layer and not the UI.

To get the tenant information through a CAP service: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/jun24#fixed-req-user-tenant

CAP Feature toggles: https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/extensibility/feature-toggles

entry.title: Re: Fiori WebGUI/WebDynpro/URL tile opens in the incorrec...
entry.content: Could be other reasons as well, like url parameter, browser language, .. see more details in this post: Solved: Fiori Launchpad Does not consider SU01 default Lan... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Sales Invoices for Export sales with payment of IGST...
entry.content:
Hi @Bhavesh_Bhorde, 

If we need a check box to control the IGST tax, we can create a custom field (check box) in the Sales: Sales Document and then activate the Business content (Sales: Pricing Communication Head), then add this custom field in Sales pricing catalog in the SSCUI and then create a condition table same as the standard or use different fields and then use the now tables in the IGST condition using a Z access sequence.

Then create the condition record with the Check box custom field, when there is a value in the custom field 18% and when it is blank 0%.

Hope this helps!!!

Thanks and Regards,
Suryaprakash.

entry.title: Re: SAP UI5 API Calls - No Preview Available
entry.content: Yes @WouterLemaire , seems the same issue to me. Thanks for connecting the dots. Please do post if you find anything relevant to fix it here. Appreciate your prompt response.

entry.title: Upload Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable (AR) journal entries
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Can I use Upload General Journal Entries app to upload Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable (AR) journal entries?

Thanks & Best regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Upload Accounts Payable (AP) and Accounts Receivable...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can kindly refer to the below help portal.
Upload General Journal Entries: FAQ
It says the "Upload General Journal Entries" app supports uploading G/L journal entries only.

To upload or create supplier invoices and customer open items, you can kindly try the "Import Supplier Invoices" and "Upload Customer Open Items" apps.

Import Supplier Invoices
With this app, you can import multiple supplier invoices into the system all at once.

Upload Customer Open Items
With this app, you can upload multiple customer open items into the system all at once.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Chrome v129 is bugged with $batch odata calls. Preview / response does not work
entry.content:
Hello @Noel_Hendrikx, facing the same issue on Microsoft Edge. Please keep the thread updated if you find any solution. Meanwhile i am also looking into it

 

FYR: SAP UI5 API Calls - No Preview Available - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
@AndreasForster,  I am happy to say, embedded #MachineLearning is now also available with #SAPHANACloud 𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐚𝐥 🚀 😊 !

You can sign-up for SAP HANA Cloud trial here https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hana-cloud-mission-trial-1.html

entry.title: Re: How to add custom property to FLP (Cloud Portal Service)
entry.content: Hi WouterLemaire, thanks for the detailed response. I believe the tenant information provided by CAP is not suitable for us as the tenant ids, subdomains, etc may change depending on the landscape. Also, it may be possible for multiple tenants to share the same custom logic. What I was hoping for is some way to inject a string into the FLP that the UI can access via JavaScript.

entry.title: How to hide detailed entry tab in FIORI APP My time event (F1506A)?
entry.content:
Hello,

we test the SAP FIORI APP “My time events” (F1506A). How we can hide the tab “Detailed Entry”?

Thank you very much for your support.

Best regards

Helmut

entry.title: Re: Capability of billing freight cost to customer in Embedded Transportation management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi,

we are in a delivery-based planning scenario. My question is: do we have to activate pricing in Delivery documents, or it is enough to have it on Sales Order and Billing Document to get this integration working?

Thank you,

Dani

entry.title: Re: Managing Stalled Workflows
entry.content:
@rahulpal Thanks for detail description of how to manage stalled workflows.

I want to know about Sent back workflows, for sent back workflows initiator gets 3 options 'Resubmit', 'Update' and 'Withdraw'. Is there any process in set up if no further action taken by initiator after x number of days, system should automatically cancel this request.

entry.title: Re: Adding association as input parameter for custom acti...
entry.content:
I think essentially it's doing the same thing that my approach does. You have assigned the action a valuehelp and storing the data in a string field

AuthorisationType : String(30)

 And then in the JS file you are creating a draft for that entity with the inputed data. But if you assign the action's input type a field that is an association field i.e.

someAction(inp : someEntity : associationFeildOfThatEntity)

Or any variation of this it will give you an error. The issue is that if the associated entity i.e. the entity in the Collection path of the value help has a composite key then that means that every single column has values that are repeating, And if the user tries to select multiple values , it will only let you select those that have different values in the field 

ValueListProperty

Thus ignoring some of the values .

entry.title: Re: Migrate the ASE database (On-Prem) to the SAP hana Cl...
entry.content: Hi Javier,

entry.title: How to define event types for home office in FIORI APP My time event (F1506A)?
entry.content:
Hello,

we test the SAP FIORI APP “My time events” (F1506A).

The event types P10 (clock-in) and P20 (clock-out) should only be used for office.

How we can define additional event types at home like P35 and P45? Have you any ideas of event types only for home office?

Thank you very much for your support.

Best regards

Helmut

entry.title: Re: Change outbound crossNavigation in Fiori adaption project
entry.content: Not an expert on the adaptation project but if it is the manifest changes you are looking to, it would have to be something like what is documented here: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/topic/8bd546e27a5f41cea6e251ba04534d70

entry.title: Getting issue in TDS Calcualtion, Base on LDC Certificate Exemption
entry.content:
As per the client's requirement, there are vendors who have an LDC certificate from the Income Tax Department. 

For those vendors, the TDS amount should be exempt up to a certain exemption amount.

We have added the data of Vendor master in role Supplier (Financial Accounting), tab India: TAN-Based Exemption in app Maintain Business Partner (BP) but when we are posting the Invoice, the TDS amount is not calculated as per the Exemption Rate.

Please provide the solution.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Getting issue in TDS Calcualtion, Base on LDC Certifi...
entry.content:
Ensure you enter data in each of the fields referenced in help documentation 
Maintaining TAN Exemption Reasons for Suppliers

See referenced in same the following;

"Enter the TAN exemption details, such as the section code, exemption number, exemption rate, exemption from and to dates, withholding tax types and tax codes, exemption threshold amount."

 

Ensure you enter in the exemption threshold amount.  If this is not entered exemption is not calculated.

 

See documentation in full from link above.  (In relation to customers certificates see also  Maintaining Details of Customers' Certificates)

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Meetup Porto
entry.content: very cool 🍻👏

entry.title: Re: NSQ Meetup on Thu, Sept 26 - Who is in / Where to meet?
entry.content: Nice! 👏😀

entry.title: Re: Getting issue in TDS Calcualtion, Base on LDC Certificate Exemption
entry.content: TDS Calculation

entry.title: Re: Event Processing in an SAP S/4HANA on-premise system
entry.content: create event consumption model if topics are not displayed.

entry.title: New Partner Content on SAP Business Accelerator Hub (Q3 ’24)
entry.content:
As a continuation of the previous blog, we would like to provide further updates and keep up the momentum of sharing relevant information with our community.

In this blog series, learn about the new content packages published on SAP Business Accelerator Hub from our partners during Q3 '2024, along with their high-level details.

For complete details on each partner's content, refer to the partners listing which is available on the home page of SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PriceFx AG

Integration package published:

SAP S/4HANA Agreements and Promotions Integration with Pricefx

This integration package contains integration flows to replicate Agreements and Promotions from Pricefx to SAP S/4HANA. They can be used as templates to speed up your integration between SAP S/4HANA to Pricefx. You could modify message mappings and value mappings according to your needs.

SAP S/4HANA Agreements and Promotions Mapping with Pricefx

This integration package contains sample mappings required to replicate Agreements and Promotions from SAP S/4HANA to Pricefx. You should modify or create new message mappings and value mappings according to your needs.

SAP S/4HANA Rebate Payout Records Mapping with Pricefx

This integration package contains sample mappings required to create credit/debit memos and accruals in SAP S/4HANA from Pricefx rebate payout records. You should modify or create new message mappings and value mappings according to your needs.

SAP S/4HANA Rebate Payout Records Integration with Pricefx

This integration package contains integration flows to create credit/debit memos and accruals in SAP S/4HANA from Pricefx Rebate Payout Records. They can be used as templates to speed up your integration between SAP S/4HANA and Pricefx. You could modify message mappings and value mappings according to your needs.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Sodales Solutions Inc 

Integration package published:

Third party integration with SAP Document Management Service

This integration package enables users for the dynamic creation of folders and posting of documents on a specified folder path. It also allows retrieval of documents based on their objectId.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

AVVALE ESPAÑA S.L.U

Integration package published:

b+ dgtal Observability for SAP Integration Suite

b+ dgtal Observability extends SAP Integration Suite adding extra functionalities such as automatic and central monitoring. It provides a global vision of the status of all the integrations scenarios via custom dashboards in ELK or Splunk.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Cobrainer GmbH

Integration package published:

SAP SuccessFactors Job Profile Builder Integration with Cobrainer Talent

This package contains artifacts that enable the following integration scenarios between SAP SuccessFactors Job Profile Builder and Cobrainer Talent.

Cobrainer Reusable Artifacts for SAP SuccessFactors Integration

This package contains reusable and helper artifacts used across the different integration scenarios between SAP SuccessFactors and Cobrainer Products.

SAP SuccessFactors Reusable Artifacts for Cobrainer Integration

This package contains reusable artifacts/Integration Flows directly interacting with SAP SuccessFactors APIs. These are reused across the different integration scenarios between SAP SuccessFactors and Cobrainer Products.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NTT DATA Business Solutions Ltd

SAP-validated partner use case (VPUC) published:

AI Inspection Assistant

Intelligent generation of Maintenance Notifications during the Inspection process to improve speed to fix and release engineering talent from repetitive activity.

User Experience Feedback Assistant

The User Experience Feedback Assistant benefits medium to large enterprises across various industries utilizing SAP S/4HANA Cloud, particularly targeting IT teams and Application Managed Service providers.

Vendor AI Vetting and Onboarding

Vendor Collaboration Simplified using AI and Automation to simplify the management of new and existing suppliers in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Using Business AI every new request is properly appraised and analysed against critical vetting criteria.

Customer Service Product Similarity Search

Enable customers to explore extended catalogue to match your products to those found elsewhere and receive the right recommendation, all embedded in an end to end email workflow.

Smart Requisitioning

Smart Requisitioning gives a guided experience to non-core SAP users in the wider organisation, using Business AI to recommend those materials and services that may be required to meet their objectives.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PiLog India Pvt Ltd

API package published:

PiLog Auto Classification

PiLog automation process brings classification for Material and Service Master Records into your organization with Consistency and efficiency reducing time and effort.Classification helps users in grouping of similar products and services under a single category which will have awareness of the products and services giving company-wide visibility of spend analysis.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SirionLabs Pte Ltd

Integration package published:

SAP CPQ Integration with Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM)

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) sell side adapter for SAP CPQ seamlessly integrates the contracting flow to the sell side process. New quotes created on SAP CPQ are automatically transitioned into a Contract Request on Sirion CLM enabling a sync between two systems for the entire sales and contracting flow.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are interested in Partnering with SAP Business Accelerator Hub, please refer to this section and discover center mission for more details. 

Stay tuned for further updates and information by the end of the next quarter!

entry.title: Re: Manual Outgoing/Incoming payment in s4 Hana
entry.content:
There are many ways to resolve this query. One of the simple is to do enhancement using ABAP.

you can take help from your abaper.

sample code as bellow. it may help you.

IF_FINS_GL_ACCOUNT_SERVICES~GET_COMPANY_CODE_DATA

  METHOD if_fins_gl_account_services-->get_company_code_data.
    rs_skb1 = ms_account_details_skb1.

ENHANCEMENT 1  ZF28.    "active version
IF SY-TCODE EQ 'FBZ1'.
   RS_SKB1-SAKNR = 'XXXXXXXX1'.
ENDIF.
IF SY-TCODE EQ 'FBZ2'.
   RS_SKB1-SAKNR = 'XXXXXXXX2'.
ENDIF.
ENDENHANCEMENT.

  ENDMETHOD.
